task_completion.description: Vital Recorder application is open on the desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The longest case (0002.vital) is loaded into Vital Recorder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder interface shows '0002.vital' loaded, with its physiological tracks displayed.

task_completion.description: Event markers are used to identify the perioperative phases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event markers list or timeline (showing 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', etc.) is visible in the Vital Recorder interface.

task_completion.description: The intraoperative segment is exported to longest_case_intraop.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog saving to 'longest_case_intraop.csv', or the resulting CSV file is visible on the desktop or opened in a viewer.

task_completion.description: The phase analysis report is created with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'phase_analysis.txt' containing the case identifier (0002), total duration, phase durations in minutes, percentages, and available physiological tracks.

integrity.description: The agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to analyze the case and export data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Vital Recorder application (e.g., clicking menus, viewing event markers, using the export feature) rather than solely relying on terminal scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not hallucinate the presence of the ST_II track in case 0002.
integrity.visual_evidence: The phase_analysis.txt report does not list 'ST_II' among the available tracks for case 0002, as it is not present in the actual file.

integrity.description: The report contains calculated values rather than placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The phase_analysis.txt file contains actual numbers for durations and percentages, not generic templates or copied task instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Case 0002 (0002.vital) is the longest surgical recording at 15,740 seconds (~262 minutes). Case 0002 has 76 physiological tracks including ECG_II, PLETH, HR, ST_V5, NIBP, SpO2, and ventilator parameters. CRITICAL: Case 0002 does NOT have the ST_II track (only ST_V5 is present). The intraoperative segment is bounded by the 'Surgery started' and 'Surgery finished' event markers.
